  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Environment and Deployment Readiness - ROLE-OPS-E1-S05
Scenario: Operations and SRE completes insightful dashboards and recommendations workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Operations and SRE receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---

ROLE-OPS-E2: Observability and Event Intelligence
Epic Goal: detect and understand production issues quickly.

Story ID: ROLE-OPS-E2-S01
Epic: ROLE-OPS-E2 - Observability and Event Intelligence
User Story: As a Operations and SRE, I want a role-specific workspace so that I can complete daily work with fewer clicks and less context switching.
UX Focus: Optimize the Operations and SRE journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Observability and Event Intelligence - ROLE-OPS-E2-S01
Scenario: Operations and SRE completes a role-specific workspace workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Operations and SRE receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions

Story ID: ROLE-OPS-E4-S04
Epic: ROLE-OPS-E4 - Automation and Runbook Execution
User Story: As a Operations and SRE, I want automated quality and policy checks so that I can detect issues before they propagate downstream.
UX Focus: Optimize the Operations and SRE journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Automation and Runbook Execution - ROLE-OPS-E4-S04
Scenario: Operations and SRE completes automated quality and policy checks workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Operations and SRE receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---

ROLE-OPS-E5: Reliability Optimization
Epic Goal: improve reliability with data-driven SRE practices.

Story ID: ROLE-OPS-E5-S01
Epic: ROLE-OPS-E5 - Reliability Optimization
User Story: As a Operations and SRE, I want a role-specific workspace so that I can complete daily work with fewer clicks and less context switching.
UX Focus: Optimize the Operations and SRE journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.

  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.

  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.

ROLE-SEC: Security Experience
Role Objective: Deliver a role-specific user experience layer on top of platform capabilities for Security.

ROLE-SEC-E1: Policy and Control Definition
Epic Goal: define enforceable security controls.

Story ID: ROLE-SEC-E1-S01
Epic: ROLE-SEC-E1 - Policy and Control Definition
User Story: As a Security, I want a role-specific workspace so that I can complete daily work with fewer clicks and less context switching.
UX Focus: Optimize the Security journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):

  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record

  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Threat and Vulnerability Management - ROLE-SEC-E2-S02
Scenario: Security completes guided artifact creation workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Security receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---
Story ID: ROLE-SEC-E2-S03
Epic: ROLE-SEC-E2 - Threat and Vulnerability Management
User Story: As a Security, I want collaboration and handoff support so that I can handoff work without losing intent or constraints.
UX Focus: Optimize the Security journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):

  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Security receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---

  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Security Posture Insights - ROLE-SEC-E5-S01
Scenario: Security completes a role-specific workspace workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Security receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---
Story ID: ROLE-SEC-E5-S02
Epic: ROLE-SEC-E5 - Security Posture Insights
User Story: As a Security, I want guided artifact creation so that I can produce high-quality outputs consistently.
UX Focus: Optimize the Security journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.

- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.

  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.

ROLE-ITSD: IT Service Desk Admin Experience
Role Objective: Deliver a role-specific user experience layer on top of platform capabilities for IT Service Desk Admin.

ROLE-ITSD-E1: Ticket Intake and Classification
Epic Goal: classify requests and incidents accurately.

Story ID: ROLE-ITSD-E1-S01
Epic: ROLE-ITSD-E1 - Ticket Intake and Classification
User Story: As a IT Service Desk Admin, I want a role-specific workspace so that I can complete daily work with fewer clicks and less context switching.
UX Focus: Optimize the IT Service Desk Admin journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Ticket Intake and Classification - ROLE-ITSD-E1-S01
Scenario: IT Service Desk Admin completes a role-specific workspace workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the IT Service Desk Admin receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions

Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Incident to Change Correlation - ROLE-ITSD-E2-S05
Scenario: IT Service Desk Admin completes insightful dashboards and recommendations workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the IT Service Desk Admin receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---

ROLE-ITSD-E3: Resolution Guidance and Handoffs
Epic Goal: route and resolve tickets with contextual guidance.

Story ID: ROLE-ITSD-E3-S01
Epic: ROLE-ITSD-E3 - Resolution Guidance and Handoffs
User Story: As a IT Service Desk Admin, I want a role-specific workspace so that I can complete daily work with fewer clicks and less context switching.
UX Focus: Optimize the IT Service Desk Admin journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Resolution Guidance and Handoffs - ROLE-ITSD-E3-S01
Scenario: IT Service Desk Admin completes a role-specific workspace workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the IT Service Desk Admin receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions

Story ID: ROLE-ITSD-E5-S04
Epic: ROLE-ITSD-E5 - Service Desk Performance Insights
User Story: As a IT Service Desk Admin, I want automated quality and policy checks so that I can detect issues before they propagate downstream.
UX Focus: Optimize the IT Service Desk Admin journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):

ROLE-GOV: Governance and Compliance Experience
Role Objective: Deliver a role-specific user experience layer on top of platform capabilities for Governance and Compliance.

ROLE-GOV-E1: Control Framework Mapping
Epic Goal: map enterprise controls to SDLC activities.

Story ID: ROLE-GOV-E1-S01
Epic: ROLE-GOV-E1 - Control Framework Mapping
User Story: As a Governance and Compliance, I want a role-specific workspace so that I can complete daily work with fewer clicks and less context switching.
UX Focus: Optimize the Governance and Compliance journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Control Framework Mapping - ROLE-GOV-E1-S01
Scenario: Governance and Compliance completes a role-specific workspace workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Governance and Compliance receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions

Story ID: ROLE-GOV-E3-S02
Epic: ROLE-GOV-E3 - Policy Compliance Monitoring
User Story: As a Governance and Compliance, I want guided artifact creation so that I can produce high-quality outputs consistently.
UX Focus: Optimize the Governance and Compliance journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize microcopy, labels, helper text, and inline guidance for first-time and expert users.
- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Policy Compliance Monitoring - ROLE-GOV-E3-S02
Scenario: Governance and Compliance completes guided artifact creation workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Governance and Compliance receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---
Story ID: ROLE-GOV-E3-S03
Epic: ROLE-GOV-E3 - Policy Compliance Monitoring
User Story: As a Governance and Compliance, I want collaboration and handoff support so that I can handoff work without losing intent or constraints.
UX Focus: Optimize the Governance and Compliance journey with fewer handoff gaps, clearer priorities, and faster decision confidence across the SDLC.
Tasks:
- Task 1: Design the interaction flow and content model.
  - Subtask 1.1: Map happy-path, edge-case, empty-state, and failure-state screens with explicit user actions.

- Task 2: Define language-agnostic contracts and event boundaries.
  - Subtask 2.1: Publish OpenAPI/AsyncAPI contracts with request, response, and error schemas.
  - Subtask 2.2: Define domain events, correlation IDs, and idempotency keys for microservice-safe orchestration.
- Task 3: Implement the Next.js frontend experience.
  - Subtask 3.1: Build responsive UI components, route handlers, and state transitions using shared design tokens.
  - Subtask 3.2: Implement client validation, keyboard accessibility, and optimistic/pessimistic update handling.
- Task 4: Implement backend service capability with extensible adapters.
  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Policy Compliance Monitoring - ROLE-GOV-E3-S03
Scenario: Governance and Compliance completes collaboration and handoff support workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Governance and Compliance receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions

  - Subtask 4.1: Implement stateless endpoint/service logic with clear domain boundaries and retry-safe behavior.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add adapter interfaces for future Python, data, AI, plugin, tool, and utility services.
- Task 5: Verify behavior, reliability, and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and end-to-end tests mapped directly to acceptance criteria.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry for latency, errors, abandonment, and completion to a role-specific dashboard.
Success Criteria (Gherkin):
Feature: Governance Improvement Loop - ROLE-GOV-E5-S05
Scenario: Governance and Compliance completes insightful dashboards and recommendations workflow
  Given the user has required permissions and connected context sources
  When the user completes the workflow in the platform
  Then the Governance and Compliance receives validated outputs, traceability links, and next-step recommendations
  And the system stores a traceable audit/event record
  And the user receives clear confirmation with next recommended actions
---


====================
QUALITY GATES FOR USING THIS BACKLOG
====================
- Every story must map to at least one measurable UX metric (time-to-complete, error rate, abandonment, satisfaction).
- Every story must be testable through the included Gherkin scenario and mapped test cases.
- Every API/event contract must be language-agnostic and microservice-ready.
- Every role story must consume platform capabilities rather than duplicate platform logic.
- Every production-impacting flow must emit audit and observability events.

Final Story Count: 500
